meta.title: Narrative Arc · Grade 7 · Sarah's Year Around the World
meta.purpose: סיפור-על דק שמלווה את 8 ה-Units לאורך השנה. דמות אחת = Sarah. כל Unit = מקום אחר. ה-narrative הוא enrichment שמופיע ב-content של חלק מהפריטים — לא mechanical, לא חייב להיות בכל פריט.

character.name: Sarah
character.background: Israeli girl on a year-long international student exchange program
character.voice: first person ('I') in writing tasks · third person ('Sarah') in reading texts
design_notes: Sarah היא דמות שמלווה את התלמיד.ה. הילד.ה לא משחק.ת את Sarah — הילד.ה היא הצופה.
ילד.ת ז' יכול.ה להזדהות עם בת 12 שיוצאת לעולם — זה fits aspirational fantasy של גיל ההתבגרות.
Sarah ישראלית = grounding לקהל יעד. חוזרת הביתה ב-Unit 7 = sense of return.

locations.name: Tel Aviv (departing)
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah is packing her suitcase. She's about to leave for a year abroad. The Bridge weeks are her preparation — phonics + sight words + classroom language she'll need.
use_in_items: Listening: 'Sarah at the airport — what does she pack?'
Reading: 'Goodbye note from Sarah's friend Maya'

locations.name: London
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah arrives in London. She meets her host family. She has to introduce herself for the first time in English.
use_in_items: Speaking: 'Sarah introduces herself to her host sister Emma'
Writing: 'Sarah's first message home — 20 words'
Reading: 'Lost & Found notice at London Heathrow'

locations.name: Mumbai
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah travels to Mumbai for Diwali to visit her host family's relatives. She learns about a big extended family living together.
use_in_items: Vocabulary: family members in a multi-generation Indian household
Reading: 'Flyer — House for rent in Mumbai (3 bedrooms, near park)'
Writing: 'Sarah's family tree drawing — host cousins'

locations.name: Tokyo
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah spends a term at a Tokyo middle school. She discovers Japanese school customs — cleaning classrooms, bento boxes, club activities.
use_in_items: Reading: 'Tokyo school timetable' — main Tnufa-aligned text type
Speaking: 'What's your favorite subject?' — comparing Tokyo and Israel
Writing: 'My dream school — 25 words'

locations.name: Barcelona
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah's host family in Barcelona has a different daily rhythm — siesta in the afternoon, late dinner, weekend walks on La Rambla.
use_in_items: Speaking: 'My morning in Barcelona — 5 sentences'
Reading: 'How to make a tortilla' instructions
Writing: 'Sarah's diary entry — Tuesday in Barcelona'

locations.name: Mexico City
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah discovers Mexican cuisine — tacos, tamales, mole. She learns to express preferences in English while trying new food.
use_in_items: Listening: 'Carlos (host brother) describes his favorite food'
Reading: 'Restaurant advertisement — La Cocina de Abuela' — Tnufa text type
Writing: 'Letter to friend in Israel — about Mexican food (25-30 words)'

locations.name: New York
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah explores New York. She learns to read subway maps, give directions, find her way in a big city.
use_in_items: Speaking: 'Describe your classroom using prepositions' (Sarah's NYC school)
Reading: 'Flyer — Free concert at Central Park' + subway map
Writing: 'My neighborhood in New York — 25 words'
Listening: 'Walking directions from Times Square to Bryant Park'

locations.name: Tel Aviv (returning)
locations.narrative_hook: Sarah comes home. She works on her Year Project — a Mini-magazine summarizing her year. Pen-pal letters to friends she made in each country.
use_in_items: Speaking: 'My favorite memory from each country'
Writing: 'Letter to my pen-pal in [country] — 30 words'
Reading: 'Blog post — A Year Abroad by Sarah Cohen' (חשיפה לכיתה ח')
Presentation: Mini-magazine pages — one per country

design_principles: Story arc is ENRICHMENT — most items work without narrative_context. Use Sarah in ~30-40% of items for atmospheric grounding.
Don't FORCE narrative on technical items (grammar drills, sight-word recognition). Save Sarah for can-do statements with real-world context.
Character is GROUND, not BURDEN — kids should feel 'Oh, Sarah's in Mumbai now' as a side-pleasure, not as a story they must follow.
Locations chosen for: (a) Linguistic exposure variety · (b) Cultural diversity · (c) Tnufa text-type relevance · (d) Israeli grounding (start + end in Tel Aviv).
End-of-year Mini-magazine project = capstone that ties all 8 locations together. Real-world output.
